Defines functions and constants for erasing and writing to flash memory with progress callbacks.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides functionality for managing flash memory on an STM32F4 microcontroller. It defines constants for the base addresses of various flash memory sectors, which are used to identify specific regions of the flash memory. The file includes two primary functions: [`system_flash_erase`](<#system_flash_erase>) and [`system_flash_write`](<#system_flash_write>). These functions allow for erasing and writing data to the flash memory, respectively. 

The [`system_flash_erase`](<#system_flash_erase>) function erases flash memory sectors within a specified address range. If any part of a sector falls within the range, the entire sector is erased. The [`system_flash_write`](<#system_flash_write>) function writes data to the flash memory, which must be erased beforehand. Both functions accept a callback function, `SystemFlashProgressCb`, which can be used to track the progress of the operations. This callback is invoked at the start and during the process, providing updates on the progress. The functions return a boolean value indicating success or failure of the operation.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `string.h`
- `stm32f4xx_flash.h`
